Title: Complete biosynthetic pathway elucidation of saponarioside B in Saponaria officinalis

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Potential anticancer activity; endosomal escape enhancement for targeted tumor therapies; potential immunostimulant activity
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Flowers and flower buds highest overall; SpB highest in young and old leaves
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Catalyzes 3-O-glucuronidation of quillaic acid, producing QA-Mono and enabling downstream SpB biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly coexpressed with SobAS1; prioritized from flower-enriched candidates
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT-DEST1 transient expression system; specific promoter not stated
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Coexpression-guided candidate selection; transient expression in N. benthamiana with SobAS1, CYP716A379 and CYP72A984; LC-MS and MS/MS comparison to authentic QA-Mono standard
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SoCSL1 is a CsyGT/CSLM family enzyme coexpressed with SobAS1. 2. Coexpression with the minimal QA pathway generated 3-O-glucuronidated QA (QA-Mono). 3. This step establishes the first sugar of the C-3 chain and channels flux toward QA-derived saponins.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: BioProject PRJNA1008697; RNA-seq/MeJa-related dataset: PRJNA1035542; genome: PRJNA1018723
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Also may glucuronidate putative 16α-hydroxygypsogenic acid-related substrate, but QA-Mono is the major product
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient heterologous expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as an essential glycosylation module for heterologous reconstruction of QA-derived saponins and pathway engineering toward SpB-like compounds

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Potential anticancer activity; endosomal escape enhancement for targeted tumor therapies; potential immunostimulant activity
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Flowers and flower buds highest overall; SpB highest in young and old leaves
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly increases production of the C-28 fucosylated intermediate QA-TriF by reducing the transferred 4-keto-6-deoxy-glucose to d-fucose in situ
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strong coexpression with SobAS1 (PCC = 0.941) and high absolute expression in flower
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT-DEST1 transient expression system; specific promoter not stated
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Coexpression analysis; transient coexpression with UGT74CD1 and QA-Tri pathway genes; LC-MS quantification showing significant increase of QA-TriF accumulation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SoSDR1 is a flower-expressed SDR strongly coexpressed with SobAS1. 2. Coexpression with UGT74CD1 markedly increases QA-TriF production. 3. Results support a mechanism in which UGT74CD1 transfers 4-keto-6-deoxy-glucose that is then reduced in situ by SoSDR1 to d-fucose.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: BioProject PRJNA1008697; RNA-seq/MeJa-related dataset: PRJNA1035542; genome: PRJNA1018723
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Predicted SDR shared 57.2% amino acid identity with QsFucSyn from Quillaja saponaria
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient heterologous expression in N. benthamiana leaves; MeJa induced expression in S. officinalis
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important enabling module for efficient fucosylation branch reconstruction in heterologous saponin engineering

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Potential anticancer activity; endosomal escape enhancement for targeted tumor therapies; potential immunostimulant activity
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Flowers and flower buds highest overall; SpB highest in young and old leaves
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Adds d-quinovose to the d-fucose moiety of QA-TriFRXX to produce QA-TriF(Q)RXX, a direct precursor to SpB
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves; recombinant protein produced in N. benthamiana
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT-DEST1 transient expression system; specific promoter not stated
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Cytosol; also observed in nucleus by passive diffusion
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GH1 transglycosidase (At/Os6 subfamily); lacks N-terminal signal peptide
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient coexpression in N. benthamiana with upstream pathway genes; LC-MS/MS and comparison to authentic standard; partial NMR support after large-scale purification; confocal localization of SoGH1:mRFP; in vitro enzyme assays with purified His-tagged protein and acyl-glucoside donors
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SoGH1 is a noncanonical GH1 transglycosidase required for quinovosylation of the C-28 sugar chain. 2. It localizes to the cytosol rather than the vacuole and lacks the typical N-terminal signal peptide of related GH1 TGs. 3. In vitro, it accepts acyl-glucosides rather than UDP-glucose, supporting an acyl-sugar donor mechanism.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: BioProject PRJNA1008697; RNA-seq/MeJa-related dataset: PRJNA1035542; genome: PRJNA1018723
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Produced two isomeric products (12 and 12′); 12 matched authentic QA-TriF(Q)RXX standard by RT and MS/MS while 12′ is likely a positional isomer
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient heterologous expression in N. benthamiana leaves; in vitro assays with benzoyl-, coumaroyl-, feruloyl-, and flavonoid-glucosides as donors
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High value for engineering rare quinovosylated saponins in heterologous hosts due to unusual donor flexibility and unique catalytic step

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Potential anticancer activity; endosomal escape enhancement for targeted tumor therapies; potential immunostimulant activity
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Flowers and flower buds highest overall; SpB highest in young and old leaves
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acetylates the d-quinovose moiety of QA-TriF(Q)RXX to form SpB; also yields SO1699 isomeric/product variant
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Candidate BAHD acyltransferase shortlisted by coexpression with SobAS1
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT-DEST1 transient expression system; specific promoter not stated
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient coexpression with full upstream pathway in N. benthamiana; LC-MS/MS comparison with authentic SpB standard; NMR-based identification of companion product SO1699 from plant material
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SoBAHD1 catalyzes the terminal acetylation step that completes SpB biosynthesis. 2. Coexpression generated a product matching authentic SpB by RT, mass and MS/MS. 3. The enzyme also yielded a second product identified as SO1699, indicating pathway branching or alternative acceptor geometry.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: BioProject PRJNA1008697; RNA-seq/MeJa-related dataset: PRJNA1035542; genome: PRJNA1018723
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: 13′ formed alongside SpB was identified as SO1699, a structurally related saponin and possible precursor to SO1861
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient heterologous expression in N. benthamiana leaves; MeJa induced expression in S. officinalis
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Terminal tailoring enzyme for producing pharmaceutically relevant acetylated saponins and analogs in engineered hosts

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes 2,3-oxidosqualene to the pentacyclic triterpene β-amyrin
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms upstream scaffold precursor for quillaic acid and ultimately SpB
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key entry/committed scaffold-forming step for the pathway
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in all soapwort organs, highest absolute expression in flower
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves; endogenous role tested in S. officinalis hairy roots
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phylogenetic analysis; transient expression in N. benthamiana with AstHMGR; GC-MS comparison to β-amyrin standard; RNAi silencing in S. officinalis hairy roots with metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome: PRJNA1018723; RNA-seq: PRJNA1008697; MeJa/RNA-seq: PRJNA1035542
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Potential anticancer activity; endosomal escape enhancement for targeted tumor therapies; potential immunostimulant activity
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Flowers and flower buds highest overall; SpB highest in young and old leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for β-amyrin formation and for accumulation of QA and SpB in planta
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT-DEST1 transient expression system; RNAi in hairy roots via pK7WGIGW-2R
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: MeJa induces expression; SobAS1 silencing in hairy roots decreases QA and SpB
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential entry enzyme for heterologous production of QA-derived saponins; strong engineering target for pathway reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing causes reduced triterpene/saponin accumulation; broader sterol-flux effects not reported
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Originally Saoffv11027757m; Saoffv11003490m was considered unlikely candidate/pseudogene-like
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SobAS1 was identified as the functional β-amyrin synthase in S. officinalis. 2. It converts 2,3-oxidosqualene to β-amyrin in N. benthamiana. 3. RNAi silencing in soapwort hairy roots significantly reduced QA and SpB, confirming in planta function.

Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Early oxidation step downstream of β-amyrin
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces a carboxylic acid residue at C-28 of β-amyrin via oxidative conversion to oleanolic acid
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms an oxidized aglycone intermediate in the route to quillaic acid and SpB
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Coexpressed with SobAS1 and shortlisted from flower-expressed candidates
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient coexpression with SobAS1; GC-MS and comparison to oleanolic acid standard
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Potential anticancer activity; endosomal escape enhancement for targeted tumor therapies; potential immunostimulant activity
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Flowers and flower buds highest overall; SpB highest in young and old leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Converts β-amyrin to oleanolic acid, establishing C-28 oxidation chemistry for downstream QA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient heterologous expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful alternative C-28 oxidase module for QA-related triterpene engineering
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Near-complete conversion of β-amyrin to oleanolic acid was observed
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP716A378 oxidizes β-amyrin at C-28. 2. Coexpression with SobAS1 caused strong accumulation of oleanolic acid. 3. It provides one route to the C-28-oxidized scaffold used in the pathway.

Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Early/mid oxidation step downstream of β-amyrin
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Carries out C-28 oxidation and additional C-16α hydroxylation of β-amyrin-derived scaffold to form echinocystic acid
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor to quillaic acid
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key multifunctional oxidation step but not explicitly designated rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Coexpressed with SobAS1 and shortlisted from flower-expressed candidates
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient coexpression with SobAS1; GC-MS and comparison to echinocystic acid standard
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Potential anticancer activity; endosomal escape enhancement for targeted tumor therapies; potential immunostimulant activity
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Flowers and flower buds highest overall; SpB highest in young and old leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces echinocystic acid, the key immediate precursor used with CYP72A984 to generate quillaic acid
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient heterologous expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value multifunctional oxidase for reducing the number of genes needed in engineered quillaic acid pathways
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Dual-function enzyme that also carries C-28 oxidation activity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP716A379 is a dual-function oxidase acting on β-amyrin-derived scaffold. 2. It forms echinocystic acid by introducing C-28 and C-16α oxidation. 3. This makes it the minimal partner with SobAS1 and CYP72A984 for QA production.

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Echinocystic acid (and possibly residual oleanolic acid or QA)
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes the C-23 position of echinocystic acid to form quillaic acid; may also further oxidize related substrates to gypsogenic acid derivatives
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms the aglycone quillaic acid used for all downstream glycosylation steps toward SpB
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Coexpressed with SobAS1 and shortlisted from flower-expressed candidates
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient coexpression with SobAS1 and CYP716A379; LC-MS/MS comparison to authentic QA standard
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Potential anticancer activity; endosomal escape enhancement for targeted tumor therapies; potential immunostimulant activity
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Flowers and flower buds highest overall; SpB highest in young and old leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Forms quillaic acid, the direct aglycone precursor for SpB biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient heterologous expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential for reconstructing QA-derived saponins in heterologous systems
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Also generated a putative gypsogenic acid peak and hydroxylated QA-related product, suggesting broader oxidative capacity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP72A984 catalyzes C-23 oxidation to yield quillaic acid. 2. Coexpression with SobAS1 and CYP716A379 generated a product matching authentic QA. 3. It may also perform additional oxidations on related intermediates.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a galactose residue to the glucuronic acid of QA-Mono to form the C-3 disaccharide QA-Di
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Extends the C-3 sugar chain required for SpB formation
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 UDP-dependent glycosyltransferase, UGT73 group D
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Candidate screening by coexpression; transient coexpression with QA-Mono-producing gene set; LC-MS/MS comparison to authentic QA-Di standard
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Potential anticancer activity; endosomal escape enhancement for targeted tumor therapies; potential immunostimulant activity
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Flowers and flower buds highest overall; SpB highest in young and old leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Adds the galactose moiety to the C-3 sugar chain, converting QA-Mono to QA-Di
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient heterologous expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Required C-3 glycosylation enzyme for reconstructing soapwort-type saponin oligosaccharides
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Phylogenetically related to UGT73CC6 and grouped in GT1 group D
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT73DL1 extends the C-3 sugar chain of QA-Mono. 2. It produces QA-Di by addition of a galactose residue. 3. This establishes the second sugar of the C-3 trisaccharide in SpB.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a xylose residue to QA-Di to complete the C-3 trisaccharide chain
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Completes the C-3 sugar chain required for downstream C-28 decoration and SpB formation
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 UDP-dependent glycosyltransferase, UGT73 group D
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient coexpression with UGT73DL1 and QA-Mono pathway genes; LC-MS/MS comparison to authentic QA-Tri standard
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Potential anticancer activity; endosomal escape enhancement for targeted tumor therapies; potential immunostimulant activity
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Flowers and flower buds highest overall; SpB highest in young and old leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Adds terminal xylose to generate QA-Tri, the completed C-3 trisaccharide intermediate
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient heterologous expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Needed to complete the soapwort C-3 trisaccharide for heterologous saponin production
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Grouped with UGT73DL1 and UGT73M2 within GT1 group D
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT73CC6 elongates the C-3 sugar chain of QA-Di. 2. It adds a xylose residue to form QA-Tri. 3. This completes the C-3 glycosylation module before C-28 sugar-chain assembly.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a 4-keto-6-deoxy-glucose-derived moiety to the C-28 carboxyl of QA-Tri, which is subsequently reduced to d-fucose by SoSDR1
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Initiates the C-28 sugar chain essential for SpB biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 UDP-dependent glycosyltransferase, GT1 group L ester-forming UGT
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient coexpression with QA-Tri-producing gene set; LC-MS/MS comparison to authentic QA-TriF standard; enhanced accumulation upon coexpression with SoSDR1
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Potential anticancer activity; endosomal escape enhancement for targeted tumor therapies; potential immunostimulant activity
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Flowers and flower buds highest overall; SpB highest in young and old leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Initiates C-28 sugar chain formation leading to the fucosylated intermediate QA-TriF
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient heterologous expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Critical enzyme for introducing the unusual fucose-derived ester-linked C-28 sugar chain
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: QA-TriF accumulated at low levels without SoSDR1, indicating donor or reductive bottleneck
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Likely transfers 4-keto-6-deoxy-glucose rather than preformed UDP-d-fucose
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT74CD1 initiates C-28 glycosylation of QA-Tri. 2. The product matches authentic QA-TriF but accumulates weakly alone. 3. Coexpression with SoSDR1 strongly supports an in situ reduction mechanism to d-fucose.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Adds a deoxyhexose, putatively L-rhamnose, to the fucose moiety at C-28 to form QA-TriFR
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Extends the C-28 sugar chain toward SpB
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 UDP-dependent glycosyltransferase, GT1 group A
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient coexpression with QA-TriF pathway genes; LC-MS/MS of new product consistent with QA-TriFR
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Potential anticancer activity; endosomal escape enhancement for targeted tumor therapies; potential immunostimulant activity
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Flowers and flower buds highest overall; SpB highest in young and old leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Adds the rhamnose residue to the C-28 chain, converting QA-TriF to QA-TriFR
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient heterologous expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Required for elaborating the characteristic linear C-28 oligosaccharide in soapwort saponins
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Near-conversion of QA-TriF to QA-TriFR was observed
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT79T1 adds a deoxyhexose to QA-TriF. 2. MS/MS supports placement on the C-28 fucose moiety, yielding QA-TriFR. 3. It is a core elongation enzyme of the C-28 chain.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a xylose residue to QA-TriFR, forming QA-TriFRX
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Builds the linear C-28 tetrasaccharide branch en route to SpB
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 UDP-dependent glycosyltransferase, GT1 group A
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient coexpression with upstream pathway genes; depletion of QA-TriFR and accumulation of QA-TriFRX detected by LC-MS/MS
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Potential anticancer activity; endosomal escape enhancement for targeted tumor therapies; potential immunostimulant activity
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Flowers and flower buds highest overall; SpB highest in young and old leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Adds the first xylose of the distal C-28 xylosyl extension, producing QA-TriFRX
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient heterologous expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for constructing the distal xylose branch on C-28 sugar chains in engineered saponins
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: MS/MS indicates elongation occurs on C-28 sugar chain rather than aglycone
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT79L3 converts QA-TriFR to QA-TriFRX by pentose addition. 2. Fragmentation patterns place this modification on the C-28 chain. 3. It introduces the first distal xylose of the linear C-28 extension.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a terminal xylose residue to QA-TriFRX, generating QA-TriFRXX
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms the immediate acceptor for quinovosylation in the route to SpB
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 UDP-dependent glycosyltransferase, UGT73 group D
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient coexpression with UGT79L3 and upstream pathway genes; LC-MS/MS detection of QA-TriFRXX
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Potential anticancer activity; endosomal escape enhancement for targeted tumor therapies; potential immunostimulant activity
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Flowers and flower buds highest overall; SpB highest in young and old leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Adds the terminal xylose to complete the main linear C-28 sugar chain precursor before quinovosylation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient heterologous expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key terminal xylosyltransferase for generating the direct precursor of quinovosylated saponins
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors note enzyme order in planta may be networked/partially flexible relative to SoGH1 action
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT73M2 adds the terminal xylose to QA-TriFRX. 2. The resulting product QA-TriFRXX is the direct precursor for SoGH1-mediated quinovosylation. 3. This completes the main linear C-28 sugar chain present in SpA/SpB.
